mnemonics: Mean, Median, Mode - The 3 M's of Location. Remember them like a 'Triple M' rating for the center of your data.
Standard Deviation Spread: Picture a 'deviant' who stands out from the crowd. The more deviants, the wider the spread of the crowd (data).
keyPoints: The mean is the average value and is sensitive to extreme outliers.
The median is the middle value and is a robust estimate of location, resistant to outliers.
The standard deviation measures the average distance of each data point from the mean, quantifying the spread or variability of the data.
commonMistakes: Using the mean instead of the median for skewed distributions, which gives a misleading 'center'.
Assuming data is normally distributed and interpreting standard deviation without checking the distribution first.
